topic: CMB Axis of Evil — Cycle 3 change-driven re-review (detail_verdict_text expanded +350%, detail_evidence shrunk after duplication fix)
change_summary: detail_verdict_length grew from 289 to 1305 (+350%) per drift-audit (2026-05-05T20:52Z, age 73.7h) — analyst expansion added a full post-hoc-test paragraph referencing Land & Magueijo 2005, dome's own definition of prospective, and our Part 3.1b carve-out. detail_evidence_length shrank from 3341 to 2431 (-27%, c2-flagged duplication bug fixed). Picked from drift-audit.json bucket=large_rewrite, max_drift_pct=333.2, position 9 in candidate list; obe_skipped=9 (WIN-050/028/009/036/060/018/059/057/023 already re-reviewed since audit).
our_argument_summary: WIN-048 claims the CMB Axis of Evil supports the dome via a ΛCDM counter-prediction framing. Our review attacks on six fronts: (1) false dilemma (ΛCDM anomaly ≠ dome evidence), (2) Planck reanalysis erodes significance to 2-3σ / p-values 0.5-5%, (3) WIN-034 copper firmament opacity self-contradiction (skin depth ~0.16 μm at 160 GHz), (4) no dome mechanism for CMB generation in flat geometry, (5) firmament thermal emission cannot reproduce acoustic peaks/polarization/lensing, (6) post-hoc test failure under our standard (Land & Magueijo 2005 was published two decades before V51.0). Cycle 3 confirms the c2 duplication bug is fixed and the analyst expansion added rigorous post-hoc framing in detail_verdict_text. Verdict: Misleading.

relevant_files: raw-text/08-live-power.txt:248-250 — single static dashboard entry: 'Domain: CMB Axis of Evil / Predicted: >2σ / Observed: 2.5σ / Tolerance: N/A / Status: PASS / Source: Planck 2018'
raw-text/02-wins.txt — WIN-048 listed in WIN catalog; no monitoring or fetch logic
(no monitor.py file present in raw-text/ in this snapshot — the dome's automation scripts are not committed to the cloned mirror; this is a project-wide caveat, not specific to WIN-048)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitoring code is present in raw-text/. The live power dashboard text file shows a static entry with hardcoded predicted (>2σ) and observed (2.5σ) values. No fetch, no comparison logic. The >2σ threshold was set after the 2.5σ Planck result was known — a textbook post-hoc threshold.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Static display only. The >2σ threshold is post-hoc-set against a known 2.5σ result. Tolerance 'N/A' admits no quantitative comparison logic exists.
data_sources_used: None — 'Planck 2018' is listed as Source but no Planck data is pulled.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The >2σ threshold is maximally permissive: under any estimator that yields a non-zero result above 2σ, the dome registers PASS. Per Planck 2015 XXIII, alignment significance ranges ~1σ to ~3σ depending on estimator and mask; the dashboard threshold guarantees PASS across that range.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Cycle 3 caveat: detail_verdict_text now states 'the dome author has not included it in the monitoring suite' — this is correct ABOUT MONITOR.PY but the dome DOES list WIN-048 in the live power dashboard (08-live-power.txt:248-250). A defender could exploit the imprecision: 'The live power scoreboard IS my monitoring suite.' The detail_evidence text already addresses this implicitly by noting the lack of a derivation, but the detail_verdict_text claim should distinguish 'no monitor.py entry' (true) from 'no dashboard entry' (false). See holes_found item 2.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py file is present in raw-text/. The live power dashboard (08-live-power.txt:248-250) lists WIN-048 with static Predicted/Observed values and Tolerance: N/A — no fetch, no comparison logic. 'none' remains correct.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Anomaly hijacking, not relabeling. Standard physics does NOT explain the Axis of Evil — it IS the anomaly. The dome is not renaming a standard mechanism; it is claiming an unexplained ΛCDM tension as evidence for its own model. **The wins.json tag is currently TRUE — this is a CARRY-FORWARD ERROR from c1/c2. Both prior cycles flagged this as wrong; the patch was never applied.** This must be set to FALSE.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Land & Magueijo 2005 (PRL 95, 071301) discovered the alignment using WMAP first-year data; Planck 2013/2015/2018 confirmed and refined the analysis; the dome model first claimed it as WIN-048 in V51.0 (April 2026). The dome's '>2σ' threshold is post-hoc — set against the already-known ~2.5σ Planck result.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula connects dome geometry (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, sun_altitude=5,733 km, moon_altitude=2,534 km) to any predicted CMB alignment direction, multipole structure, or significance level. The CMB sits at cosmological distances with no dome-geometry analog. The dashboard threshold is arbitrary, not derived.

holes_found.description: **CARRY-FORWARD TAG ERROR (UNFIXED across c1, c2, and now c3)**: code_analysis.relabels_standard is still TRUE in wins.json for WIN-048. Cycle 1 (early April) flagged this as incorrect; Cycle 2 (2026-04-08T15:45Z) flagged it again, marked it MAJOR, and recommended setting to FALSE. As of this c3 review (2026-05-08), the tag is STILL TRUE. The fix requires a single boolean flip in data/wins.json. This tag inflates the Part 2b aggregate 'relabels standard physics' count by 1 across all dashboard statistics. Anomaly hijacking is logically distinct from relabeling: standard physics does not explain the Axis of Evil — there is no standard mechanism to rename. The dome is grabbing an unexplained anomaly, not renaming an existing answer.
holes_found.recommendation: Set wins.json[id=048].code_analysis.relabels_standard = false. Verify Part 2b counts re-render correctly. The tag fix is a one-character JSON edit; the prose stats in part2b will need to be recomputed (current count likely shows N relabels — should drop by 1).

holes_found.description: detail_verdict_text claim 'the dome author has not included it in the monitoring suite' is technically true of monitor.py but ignores the dome's own live power dashboard (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:248-250) which lists 'Domain: CMB Axis of Evil / Predicted: >2σ / Observed: 2.5σ / Status: PASS / Source: Planck 2018'. A dome defender will exploit the imprecision: 'The live power scoreboard IS my monitoring suite — you are cherry-picking by demanding a Python script.' The argument we want to make is stronger if precise: yes, there IS a dashboard entry, but its >2σ threshold was chosen post-hoc against a known 2.5σ result, the tolerance is N/A, and no Planck data is fetched — making it a static display, not a verifying monitor.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'the dome author has not included it in the monitoring suite' with 'the only monitoring artifact for this value is a static live-power dashboard entry (Predicted: >2σ, Observed: 2.5σ, Tolerance: N/A) — a post-hoc threshold against a known result, not a fetch-and-compare monitor.' This makes the same point without giving the defender the easy retort.

holes_found.description: **Lens A finding (Advocate Mode)**: Our WIN-034 self-contradiction argument depends on a literal reading of 'cast copper/bronze' as engineering specification. A dome defender can retreat to 'WIN-034 is biblical poetry / metaphorical material — Job 37:18 uses simile, not solid-copper engineering — so a 0.16 μm skin depth is not on the dome model.' Our WIN-034 review (verdict: Unfalsifiable) actually concedes this is biblical exegesis. We rely on the dome's own use of WIN-034 as evidence for WIN-029 (Schumann conductive ceiling) to force the literal reading. WIN-048 detail_evidence does not preempt this retreat.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence after the WIN-034 paragraph in detail_evidence: 'A dome defender may retreat to claiming WIN-034 is metaphorical, but the dome model itself uses WIN-034 to provide the conductive ceiling required by WIN-029 Schumann claim — the firmament cannot be metaphorical for one purpose and physical for another.' This neutralizes the easiest escape.

holes_found.description: **Carry-forward from c2 (still not integrated)**: The ecliptic alignment paradox is not in the current detail_evidence. The CMB quadrupole-octopole alignment correlates with the ecliptic plane — Earth's orbital plane around the Sun. In dome geometry the ecliptic has no physical meaning (no heliocentric orbit). If the dome claims the alignment as evidence, it must explain why a dome anomaly correlates with a frame defined by the heliocentric model the dome rejects. This is a dilemma argument: either coincidental (reduces dome's significance) or real (supports heliocentrism, not dome). Cycle 2 stronger_arguments flagged this; not integrated.
holes_found.recommendation: Add to detail_evidence: 'The anomalous quadrupole-octopole alignment correlates with the ecliptic plane (Schwarz et al. 2004, PRL 93, 221301) — Earth orbit around the Sun. In dome geometry the ecliptic is not defined; the dome rejects the heliocentric frame that gives this alignment its name. Either the alignment is coincidental (eroding the dome claimed significance) or it reflects a real physical link to the ecliptic (supporting heliocentrism, the model the dome opposes).'

holes_found.description: **Carry-forward from c2 (still not integrated)**: The Land & Magueijo 2005 DOI (10.1103/PhysRevLett.95.071301) — the foundational paper — is mentioned by author/year in detail_verdict_text but never linked. C2 recommended adding the citation; not done. Adding the link would strengthen citation integrity, especially since detail_verdict_text now leans on this paper to date the original observation.

Planck 2018 isotropy paper is cited generically without DOI. The standard reference is Planck Collaboration (2020), 'Planck 2018 results. VII. Isotropy and statistics of the CMB,' A&A 641, A7, DOI: 10.1051/0004-6361/201935201. Adding the link would make the 'Planck 2018 confirmed these conclusions' sentence verifiable.
holes_found.recommendation: Hyperlink 'Planck 2018 isotropy and statistics analysis' to https://doi.org/10.1051/0004-6361/201935201.

holes_found.description: **Cross-WIN cosmological cluster carry-forward (c2 stronger_argument, still not integrated)**: WINs 047, 048, 052, 054, 055 collectively reference cosmological-distance phenomena (Hubble flow, CMB, lensing, El Gordo, Cepheid distances) while the dome maxes out at ~20,015 km. None of these WINs explicitly invokes the cluster-level argument: where do these objects exist in dome geometry? The argument does not need to live in WIN-048 specifically, but it should appear somewhere in the cosmological cluster (e.g., a shared introduction in section 3.6 or in WIN-047). This was flagged in c2 stronger_arguments and remains unaddressed.
holes_found.recommendation: Out of scope for WIN-048 detail_evidence. Open as a section-level issue against part3 or part6 (kill-shots) instead.

stronger_arguments.description: Ecliptic alignment paradox (still not integrated — see hole 4).
sources: Schwarz et al. (2004), 'Is the Low-ℓ Microwave Background Cosmic?', PRL 93, 221301

stronger_arguments.description: Cross-WIN cosmological cluster incoherence (still not integrated — see hole 7).
sources: Internal consistency: WIN-047/048/052/054/055 invoke cosmological distances under a 20,015 km dome geometry without ontological accounting.

kernel_of_truth.description: The CMB Axis of Evil remains a genuinely unsettled question (data/wins.json:WIN-048.detail_extra paragraph 1). The quadrupole-octopole alignment was confirmed at Planck PR3/PR4 — it is not a WMAP artifact. Some analyses still find low-multipole anomalies significant at the 1-3% level, and ΛCDM's assumption of statistical isotropy is in mild tension with these observations. The dome author also corrected his own confidence claim from '>99.9%' down to '2-3σ' on 2026-03-23 (data/wins.json:WIN-048.detail_extra Update note), demoting the WIN to 'CONSISTENT' — a responsible self-correction that our detail_extra acknowledges.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Identifying a contested question is not making a prediction. The dome (1) provides no mechanism for CMB generation in flat geometry — see WIN-052 finding (data/wins.json:WIN-052) which makes the same charge for galaxy-scale lensing in the same cluster, (2) makes no quantitative prediction of alignment direction or angular scale, (3) derives no value from disc_radius=20,015 km / firmament_height=9,086 km, (4) cannot account for acoustic-peak structure (ℓ≈220), E-mode polarization, or gravitational lensing in CMB maps that ΛCDM does explain, (5) faces the WIN-034 opacity self-contradiction (skin-depth ≈ 0.163 μm at 160.2 GHz Wien peak — verified Lens C below), (6) cannot explain the ecliptic correlation in a frame the dome rejects (hole 4). The author's honest demotion to 2-3σ does not rescue the structural problems; lowering significance does not change copper opacity or supply a derivation.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A skilled, principled dome defender would argue: (1) You yourself credit our 2026-03-23 self-correction (>99.9% → 2-3σ) in detail_extra — that is exactly the methodological discipline you ask for, and you penalize us for engaging in it. (2) Your WIN-034 self-contradiction depends on a literal reading of 'cast copper/bronze' as solid metallic engineering — but our own WIN-034 verdict is 'Unfalsifiable' precisely because it is theological/figurative. We retain WIN-034 as evidence for a conductive cavity at the level needed for Schumann (a thin-film aetheric medium suffices), not a 0.16 μm-skin-depth solid copper wall. The 160 GHz opacity argument over-literalizes our position. (3) You claim the ΛCDM counter-prediction is a false dilemma — but ΛCDM is the model your review treats as the default. If a competing model fails to predict an observation, that is positive evidence for any alternative that does no worse, including ours. The strict logical fallacy is real but the practical inference is not: ΛCDM tension reduces our explanatory burden. (4) Your detail_verdict_text says no monitor.py entry exists — true, but the live-power dashboard at 08-live-power.txt does score this domain. You cherry-pick the strict definition of 'monitoring' to score a procedural failure.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Address all four heads. (1) Self-correction — already credited in detail_extra; the verdict text could remind readers that 2-3σ is also where the mainstream Planck consensus lives, so the corrected dome claim adds nothing new (the detail_extra already makes this point — could be cross-referenced from detail_verdict_text). (2) WIN-034 literal vs metaphorical — add the sentence in hole 3: the dome itself uses WIN-034 to supply WIN-029's conductive ceiling, forcing literalism. (3) ΛCDM tension as evidence — sharpen detail_evidence: the dome must do MORE than tie ΛCDM, it must explain features ΛCDM does explain (acoustic peaks, polarization, lensing) — failing those, the comparison is asymmetric and the 'counter-prediction' framing is invalid. (4) Monitor.py vs dashboard — fix per hole 2.

claims_checked: Copper skin depth at 160 GHz ≈ 0.2 μm
CMB peak frequency 160.2 GHz at T = 2.725 K
quantitative_verification.computations: Skin depth δ = √(2/(ωμσ)). At ω = 2π·160e9 rad/s, μ = 4π·10⁻⁷ H/m, σ = 5.96·10⁷ S/m: δ = 1.63·10⁻⁷ m = 0.163 μm. Our review says '~0.2 μm' — accurate to one significant figure. CMB Wien peak ν = 5.879·10¹⁰ × 2.725 = 160.20 GHz. Our review says '160 GHz peak frequency' — exact. p=0.005 (one-sided) ↔ z=2.576; p=0.05 (one-sided) ↔ z=1.645; p=0.005 (two-sided) ↔ z=2.807; p=0.05 (two-sided) ↔ z=1.960. Our '0.5–5%' range maps to roughly 1.6σ–2.8σ depending on convention; the dome's '2.5σ' claim sits comfortably inside this range. All three numerical claims verify.

deeper_analysis: Cycle 3 assessment: the c2 critical bug (triple-duplicated firmament-emission paragraph) is FIXED — 'thermal emission from the firmament itself' now appears exactly once in detail_evidence, and the field shrank from 3341 to 2431 chars consistent with the duplication removal. detail_verdict_text expanded from 289 → 1305 chars (+350%); the new content is a substantive post-hoc-test paragraph that cites Land & Magueijo 2005, the dome author's own definition of 'prospective,' and our Part 3.1b legitimate-novelty carve-out. The new paragraph is methodologically sound but introduces ONE imprecision (hole 2): saying the dome 'has not included it in the monitoring suite' gives a defender room to point at the live-power dashboard. The fix is one sentence. The c2 MAJOR finding — wins.json[048].code_analysis.relabels_standard still set to TRUE — survives a third cycle UNFIXED. This is now a carry-forward bug spanning three review cycles and inflating Part 2b aggregate counts. Lens A surfaces a new MODERATE finding: WIN-034's metaphorical-vs-literal status creates a clean retreat for any dome defender attacking our skin-depth argument. Our argument depends on the dome itself using WIN-034 literally for WIN-029's conductive ceiling, but WIN-048 detail_evidence does not surface that lever — adding one sentence preempts the retreat. Lens B confirms cross-WIN consistency: our argument across the cosmological cluster (047/048/052/054/055) makes the same charge — no dome ontology for cosmological-distance objects, no derivation, anomaly hijacking. Lens C verifies all three quantitative claims (skin depth, Wien peak, p-σ mapping). Verdict 'Misleading' remains correct and well-supported. recommended_action: minor_edit (the tag fix + the four text patches in holes 2–5 are all small).
